Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search for Raj Greenfield's complaint about the swapped invoice. Use search_emails(query: "Raj Greenfield") or search_emails(query: "Sunbelt pricing discrepancy") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Sunbelt", filter_in_channel: #customer-engagement). Agent finds Raj Greenfield (raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com), VP at Sunbelt Venture Partners, sent an escalation email with subject "URGENT: Pricing Discrepancy — Sunbelt Invoice (Nonprofit Pricing Header)" directly to David Chen (bypassing Emeka Diallo). He spotted the nonprofit rate header on the Palmetto invoice he received.
OE 2: Search for Carmen Delgado-Reyes's complaint about the swapped invoice. Use search_emails(query: "Carmen Delgado-Reyes") or search_emails(query: "Palmetto invoice") or search_emails(query: "INV-2026-0418-PALMETTO"). Agent finds Carmen Delgado-Reyes (carmen.delgado-reyes@palmetto-foundation.org), Operations Director at Palmetto Community Foundation, sent an email with subject "Audit Concern: Invoice Amount Mismatch (INV-2026-0418-PALMETTO)" directly to Emeka Diallo, flagging the inflated $4,800 amount as an audit risk for their nonprofit records.
OE 3: Check if anyone on MoveOps' side has responded to the client complaints. Use search_emails(query: "Emeka Diallo Sunbelt") or search_emails(query: "Emeka Palmetto") or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Emeka", filter_in_channel: #customer-engagement). Agent discovers Emeka Diallo went quiet for approximately 3 days before eventually sending an accountability message and resolution emails to both clients.
OE 4: Check internal Slack discussions about the invoice swap incident. Use conversations_search_messages(search_query: "pricing exposure", filter_in_channel: #finance) or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Sunbelt", filter_in_channel: #customer-engagement) or conversations_search_messages(search_query: "Terraform leak", filter_in_channel: #finance). Agent finds: David Chen posted an urgent escalation in #customer-engagement comparing it to the Terraform leak; Marcus Thorne flagged pricing policy exposure in #finance noting $4,800 vs $2,400; Chloe Vance identified root cause as missing invoice-attachment validation in #operations.
OE 5: Look up QuickBooks billing records to confirm correct invoice amounts for both accounts. Agent searches QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices. Agent confirms: INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT = $4,800 (standard rate) for Sunbelt Venture Partners; INV-2026-0418-PALMETTO = $2,400 (nonprofit rate) for Palmetto Community Foundation. The underlying QuickBooks records have the correct amounts — the error was the swapped PDF attachments.
OE 6: Check if any payments have been processed against the wrong amounts. Agent searches QuickBooks payment records for Sunbelt and Palmetto. Agent investigates whether either client processed a payment based on the swapped invoice figures, which would require additional refund/adjustment cleanup.
OE 7: Search Linear for the invoice-attachment validation engineering ticket. Use linear_list_issues(query: "invoice validation") or linear_list_issues(query: "attachment validation") or linear_list_issues(query: "batch send"). Agent finds ticket ENG-397: "[Safety] Add invoice PDF attachment validation to batch send process", assigned to Lena Björkström, status TODO. The ticket was deprioritized during the ExpenseBot crisis and has been elevated to P2.
OE 8: Look up contact details for client write actions. Use contacts_search_contacts(query: "Raj Greenfield") or contacts_search_contacts(query: "Carmen Delgado-Reyes"). Agent obtains email addresses: raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com and carmen.delgado-reyes@palmetto-foundation.org. Contact details may also be available from earlier email search results.
OE 9: Generate corrected invoice for Sunbelt Venture Partners. Use quickbooks_create_invoice for Sunbelt Venture Partners with the correct standard rate amount of $4,800. Agent creates a new corrected invoice replacing the erroneously sent Palmetto PDF.
OE 10: Generate corrected invoice for Palmetto Community Foundation. Use quickbooks_create_invoice for Palmetto Community Foundation with the correct nonprofit rate amount of $2,400. Agent creates a new corrected invoice replacing the erroneously sent Sunbelt PDF.
OE 11: Send correction and apology email to Raj Greenfield at Sunbelt. Use send_email(to: "raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com", subject: ..., body: ...). Agent sends an email from Hana Kim acknowledging the invoice mix-up, confirming Sunbelt's correct invoice amount of $4,800, and assuring that safeguards (attachment-validation process) are being implemented. Tone is professional and genuinely apologetic.
OE 12: Send correction and apology email to Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto. Use send_email(to: "carmen.delgado-reyes@palmetto-foundation.org", subject: ..., body: ...). Agent sends an email from Hana Kim acknowledging the invoice mix-up, confirming Palmetto's correct invoice amount of $2,400 (nonprofit rate), and assuring that safeguards are being implemented. Tone is professional and genuinely apologetic.
OE 13: Post summary in #finance channel. Use conversations_add_message(channel: #finance, text: ...). Agent posts a summary explaining: the swapped invoice PDFs between Sunbelt ($4,800 standard) and Palmetto ($2,400 nonprofit); the corrected amounts; the pricing exposure risk (Sunbelt now knows Palmetto's nonprofit rate, comparable to Terraform leak); and that Alejandro should review per Marcus's standing request for pricing sensitivity review.
OE 14: Update or add comment to Linear ticket ENG-397 referencing this incident. Use linear_create_comment(issue_id: "ENG-397", body: ...) or linear_update_issue(issue_id: "ENG-397", ...). Agent adds a comment referencing the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap as further justification for prioritizing the attachment-validation feature. If no ticket is found, agent creates one with linear_create_issue.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

# Criterion Run #1Run #2Run #3Run #4Run #5Run #6 Pass Rate
1 A corrected invoice was created in QuickBooks for Sunbelt Venture Partners with an amount of $4,800.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The agent called `quickbooks_create_invoice` with `customer_ref: 'cust_sunbelt'` and a line item of $4,800, with doc_number `INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT-CORRECTED`. The tool confirmed the invoice was created successfully.
✓Run #2The agent called `quickbooks_create_invoice` for `cust_sunbelt` with Amount=4800 and UnitPrice=4800, creating invoice INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT-CORRECTED. The tool returned success.
✓Run #3The trajectory shows a `quickbooks_create_invoice` call for `cust_sunbelt` with a line item of $4,800, generating `INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT-CORRECTED`. The tool returned a success response confirming the invoice was created.
✓Run #4The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice with customer_ref 'cust_sunbelt', doc_number 'INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT-CORRECTED', and Amount: 4800. The tool returned 'Invoice created successfully'. The correct amount of $4,800 was submitted.
✓Run #5The agent called quickbooks_create_invoice for cust_sunbelt with doc_number INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT-CORRECTED and Amount: 4800 in the line items. The API returned a successful invoice creation response.
✓Run #6The trajectory shows a `quickbooks_create_invoice` tool call for Sunbelt Venture Partners with doc_number 'INV-2026-0418-SUNBELT-CORRECTED' and an Amount of 4800, and the tool returned success with a confirmed invoice created.
2 A corrected invoice was created in QuickBooks for Palmetto Community Foundation with an amount of $2,400.
outcome

3 An email was sent to Raj Greenfield at [raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com](mailto:raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com).
outcome
 ✗✗✗✓✗✗ 17%
✗Run #1The agent sent the Sunbelt email to `carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com` (Carla Mendes, Head of Operations), not to `raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com`. No email was sent to Raj Greenfield at all.
✗Run #2The agent sent the Sunbelt email to carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com, not to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com. The correct contact for Sunbelt (Raj Greenfield) never received an email.

✓Run #4The agent called send_email with recipients: ['raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com']. The tool returned a success result with an email_id.
✗Run #5The agent sent the Sunbelt email to carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com, not raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com. Raj Greenfield was identified in the Slack messages as the one who flagged the pricing discrepancy, but the agent addressed the email to a different contact.
✗Run #6The email for Sunbelt was sent to carla.mendes@sunbeltvp.com, not to raj.greenfield@sunbeltvp.com as required. The agent contacted the wrong person at Sunbelt Venture Partners.

The Slack messages discovered during investigation referenced 'Carmen at Palmetto' as the one who received the wrong invoice, but the agent failed to surface this in the summary.
